<objective>
Bootstrap the Next.js application with Bun, install shadcn/ui components, define shared type schemas and constants, and build the app shell (navbar + footer + layout).

Purpose: Establishes the project foundation that all subsequent plans build on -- the dev server, component library, shared types, and visual shell.
Output: Running Next.js app with shadcn/ui, Zod schemas, and app shell layout.
</objective>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Bootstrap Next.js with Bun, install dependencies, initialize shadcn/ui</name>
  <files>package.json, bun.lock, tsconfig.json, next.config.ts, postcss.config.mjs, src/app/globals.css, src/app/layout.tsx, src/app/page.tsx, src/lib/utils.ts, components.json, .env.example</files>
  <read_first>
    - CLAUDE.md (tech stack versions: Next.js 15.x, TypeScript 5.7+, Bun 1.3+, Tailwind 4.x)
    - .planning/phases/01-foundation-and-proposals/01-RESEARCH.md (bootstrap sequence, standard stack versions)
    - .planning/phases/01-foundation-and-proposals/01-UI-SPEC.md (design system: default preset, Inter font, component list)
  </read_first>
  <action>
    1. Create Next.js app at project root (overwriting into existing directory):
       ```bash
       bunx create-next-app@latest . --ts --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias "@/*" --yes
       ```
       If create-next-app refuses to write into existing directory, use a temp dir and move files.

    2. Install additional dependencies:
       ```bash
       bun add zod viem @upstash/ratelimit @upstash/redis isomorphic-dompurify
       ```

    3. Initialize shadcn/ui:
       ```bash
       bunx shadcn@latest init --defaults --yes
       ```

    4. Add required shadcn components (from UI-SPEC):
       ```bash
       bunx shadcn@latest add button card input textarea badge separator skeleton label
       ```

    5. Create `.env.example` with placeholder env vars:
       ```
       # IPFS Provider (Pinata)
       PINATA_API_KEY=
       PINATA_SECRET_KEY=
       PINATA_GATEWAY_URL=

       # On-Chain (Base Sepolia)
       NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID=84532
       RPC_URL=
       DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY=
       DEPLOYMENT_BLOCK=

       # Contract Addresses (populated after deployment)
       NEXT_PUBLIC_IDENTITY_REGISTRY_ADDRESS=
       NEXT_PUBLIC_REPUTATION_REGISTRY_ADDRESS=

       # Rate Limiting (Upstash Redis)
       UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL=
       UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN=
       ```

    6. Ensure `tsconfig.json` has `"strict": true` in compilerOptions.

    7. Update `src/app/layout.tsx` to use Inter font from `next/font/google`:
       ```typescript
       import { Inter } from "next/font/google";
       const inter = Inter({ subsets: ["latin"], weight: ["400", "600"] });
       ```

    8. Configure security headers in `next.config.ts`:
       - X-Frame-Options: DENY
       - X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
       - X-DNS-Prefetch-Control: on
       - Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
       - Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
       - Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https://gateway.pinata.cloud https://*.ipfs.io; connect-src 'self' https://gateway.pinata.cloud https://sepolia.base.org https://*.upstash.io; frame-ancestors 'none'
       - Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains

    9. Create `GET /api/health` endpoint checking chain and IPFS connectivity.

    10. Verify dev server starts: `bun run dev` (check it doesn't error).
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/libardo/carlos/projects/ipe-city/agent-reviewer/.worktrees/full-vision && bun run build 2>&1 | tail -10</automated>
  </verify>

    - `tsconfig.json` contains `"strict": true`

    - `src/components/ui/button.tsx` exists (shadcn components installed)

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Create shared types, Zod schemas, constants, and environment validation</name>
  <files>src/lib/env.ts, src/lib/schemas/proposal.ts, src/lib/constants/proposal.ts</files>
  <read_first>
    - src/app/layout.tsx (verify Task 1 bootstrap completed)
    - .planning/phases/01-foundation-and-proposals/01-RESEARCH.md (Zod schema pattern, field constraints)
    - .planning/phases/01-foundation-and-proposals/01-UI-SPEC.md (status labels, budget format, copy text)
    - .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md (PROP-01 fields, PROP-03 status transitions)
  </read_first>
  <action>
    **src/lib/constants/proposal.ts:**
    ```typescript
    export const PROPOSAL_STATUS = {
      SUBMITTED: "submitted",
      EVALUATING: "evaluating",
      EVALUATED: "evaluated",
    } as const;

    export type ProposalStatus = (typeof PROPOSAL_STATUS)[keyof typeof PROPOSAL_STATUS];

    export const STATUS_LABELS: Record<ProposalStatus, string> = {
      submitted: "Submitted",
      evaluating: "Evaluating",
      evaluated: "Evaluated",
    };

    export const FIELD_LIMITS = {
      TITLE_MIN: 5,
      TITLE_MAX: 200,
      DESCRIPTION_MIN: 50,
      DESCRIPTION_MAX: 5000,
      TEAM_INFO_MIN: 10,
      TEAM_INFO_MAX: 2000,
      BUDGET_MAX: 1_000_000,
      EXTERNAL_LINKS_MAX: 5,
    } as const;

    export const SCORING_WEIGHTS = {
      TECHNICAL_FEASIBILITY: 0.25,
      IMPACT_POTENTIAL: 0.30,
      COST_EFFICIENCY: 0.20,
      TEAM_CAPABILITY: 0.25,
    } as const;

    export const DESCRIPTION_TRUNCATE_LENGTH = 120;
    ```

    NOTE: The `as const` assertions above are value-level const assertions, NOT type assertions (`as Type`). They are permitted by the project convention since they narrow literal types rather than bypassing the type system.

    **src/lib/schemas/proposal.ts:**
    ```typescript
    import { z } from "zod";
    import { FIELD_LIMITS } from "@/lib/constants/proposal";

    export const proposalSchema = z.object({
      title: z.string()
        .min(FIELD_LIMITS.TITLE_MIN, `Title must be at least ${FIELD_LIMITS.TITLE_MIN} characters`)
        .max(FIELD_LIMITS.TITLE_MAX, `Title must be at most ${FIELD_LIMITS.TITLE_MAX} characters`),
      description: z.string()
        .min(FIELD_LIMITS.DESCRIPTION_MIN, `Description must be at least ${FIELD_LIMITS.DESCRIPTION_MIN} characters`)
        .max(FIELD_LIMITS.DESCRIPTION_MAX, `Description must be at most ${FIELD_LIMITS.DESCRIPTION_MAX} characters`),
      teamInfo: z.string()
        .min(FIELD_LIMITS.TEAM_INFO_MIN, `Team info must be at least ${FIELD_LIMITS.TEAM_INFO_MIN} characters`)
        .max(FIELD_LIMITS.TEAM_INFO_MAX, `Team info must be at most ${FIELD_LIMITS.TEAM_INFO_MAX} characters`),
      budget: z.number()
        .positive("Budget must be positive")
        .max(FIELD_LIMITS.BUDGET_MAX, `Budget must be at most $${FIELD_LIMITS.BUDGET_MAX.toLocaleString()}`),
      externalLinks: z.array(
        z.string().url("Must be a valid URL")
      ).max(FIELD_LIMITS.EXTERNAL_LINKS_MAX, `Maximum ${FIELD_LIMITS.EXTERNAL_LINKS_MAX} links allowed`),
    });

    export type ProposalInput = z.infer<typeof proposalSchema>;
    ```

    **src/lib/env.ts:**
    ```typescript
    import { z } from "zod";

    const serverEnvSchema = z.object({
      PINATA_API_KEY: z.string().min(1).optional(),
      PINATA_SECRET_KEY: z.string().min(1).optional(),
      PINATA_GATEWAY_URL: z.string().url().optional(),
      RPC_URL: z.string().url().optional(),
      DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY: z.string().min(1).optional(),
    });

    const clientEnvSchema = z.object({
      NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID: z.string().default("84532"),
      NEXT_PUBLIC_IDENTITY_REGISTRY_ADDRESS: z.string().optional(),
      NEXT_PUBLIC_REPUTATION_REGISTRY_ADDRESS: z.string().optional(),
    });

    // Server env — only accessible in server components and API routes
    export function getServerEnv() {
      return serverEnvSchema.parse(process.env);
    }

    // Client env — safe to use in client components
    export function getClientEnv() {
      return clientEnvSchema.parse({
        NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID,
        NEXT_PUBLIC_IDENTITY_REGISTRY_ADDRESS: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_IDENTITY_REGISTRY_ADDRESS,
        NEXT_PUBLIC_REPUTATION_REGISTRY_ADDRESS: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_REPUTATION_REGISTRY_ADDRESS,
      });
    }
    ```

    Make env vars optional for now (contracts not deployed yet). They become required in Plan 03 when integration layer is built.
  </action>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 3: Build app shell with NavBar, Footer, and MainContent layout</name>
  <files>src/components/app-shell.tsx, src/app/layout.tsx, src/app/page.tsx, src/app/proposals/layout.tsx</files>
  <read_first>
    - src/app/layout.tsx (current state from Task 1)
    - src/components/ui/button.tsx (verify shadcn button is available)
    - .planning/phases/01-foundation-and-proposals/01-UI-SPEC.md (App Shell section: NavBar, Footer, MainContent specs, spacing, colors, responsive breakpoints)
  </read_first>
  <action>
    **src/components/app-shell.tsx:**
    Create a "use client" component that renders the full app shell as specified in UI-SPEC:

    - **NavBar**: Fixed top bar, 64px height. Left: "IPE City Grants" text (links to `/proposals`). Right: "Submit Proposal" Button (links to `/proposals/new`). Background: `hsl(0 0% 98%)` (secondary color). Border-bottom: `hsl(0 0% 90%)`.
    - **MainContent**: `<main>` element. Horizontal padding: 16px on mobile, 0 on desktop (max-width handles centering). Vertical padding: 48px top and bottom.
    - **Footer**: 48px height. Centered text: "Built for IPE City" with link to ipe.city. Muted text color `hsl(0 0% 45%)`. Border-top: `hsl(0 0% 90%)`.
    - Use semantic HTML: `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`
    - Import `Button` from `@/components/ui/button`
    - Use `Link` from `next/link` for navigation

    **src/app/layout.tsx:**
    Update root layout to:
    - Import and render `AppShell` wrapping `{children}`
    - Set metadata: title "IPE City Grants", description "AI-powered grant evaluation for IPE City"
    - Apply Inter font className to `<html>` or `<body>`
    - No auth provider (UI-04: public access)

    **src/app/page.tsx:**
    Redirect root `/` to `/proposals` using `redirect()` from `next/navigation`.

    **src/app/proposals/layout.tsx:**
    Simple pass-through layout that wraps children in a max-width container:
    ```tsx
    export default function ProposalsLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
      return <div className="mx-auto w-full max-w-3xl px-4 sm:px-0">{children}</div>;
    }
    ```
    The 768px max-width (`max-w-3xl`) matches UI-SPEC's "max-width 768px, centered" for list and detail pages.
  </action>

<threat_model>
## Trust Boundaries

| Boundary | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Client env vars | NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars exposed to browser — no secrets |
| Server env vars | API keys accessed only in server context |

## STRIDE Threat Register

| Threat ID | Category | Component | Disposition | Mitigation Plan |
|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| T-02-01 | Information Disclosure | src/lib/env.ts | mitigate | Server env vars (PINATA_API_KEY, DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY) only accessible via getServerEnv() in server components/API routes. Client env uses separate schema with only NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars. |
| T-02-02 | Tampering | .env.example | accept | Example file contains no real values. Real .env.local is gitignored by default Next.js setup. |
</threat_model>

6. App shell visible with navbar and footer
</verification>
